folio.label: 151r
folio.l: As in that hous , and ful of diligence
To doon plesaunce , and also gret costage
He nougút for gat , to ©eue the leest page
In al that hous , but after here degree
He ©af the lord , and sithen al his meyne
Whan that he coom , som manäe honest thyngö
For whicú they were as glad , of his comyngö
As foule is fayn , whan that the so~ne vp risitú
Nomore of this as now , for it suffisitú
¢ But so byfel , this Marchau~t on a day
Shope hi~ , to make redy , his array
Toward the Toun of Brugges , for to fare
To byen there , a porciou~ of ware
For whicú he hatú , to Parys sent anon
A messager , and preyed hatú daun Ioún
That he shulde come , to Seynt Denys and pleye
¢ Witú hi~ and witú his wyf , a day or tweye

note.notetxt: Double check the paraph on the left/ODR/

folio.l: Er he to Brugges wente , in alle wyse
¢ This noble Monk , of whicú I ©ow deuyse
Hatú of his Abbot , as hi~ list , licence
By cause he was a man , of heigú prudence
And eke an officere , out for to ride
To sen here graunges , and here Bernes wyde
And vn to Seynt Denys , he cometú a non
Who was so welcome , as my lord Daun Ioún
Oure dere Cosyn , ful of curtesie
Witú hi~ brougúte he , a Iubbe of Maluesie
And eke a nother , ful of fyn vernage
And volatil , as ay was his vsage
And thus , I lete he~ , ete and drynke , and pleye
This Marchau~t and this Monk , a day or tweye
¢ The thridde day , this Marchau~t vp arisitú
And on hise nedes , sadly hi~ auysitú
And vp in to his Countour hous , gotú he
To rekene witú hi~ self , wel may be
Of thilke ©er , how that it witú hi~ stod
And how that he , dispended had his good
And if that he , encressed were or noon
Hise bookes , and hise bagges many oon
He leitú biforn hi~ , on his countyngbord
Ful riche was his tresor , and his hord
For whicú ful faste , his countoå dore he shette
And eke he nolde , that noman shulde hi~ lette
Of hise acountes , for the mene tyme
